description: Flight N1156P — KPHX-local — 1.8h — Engine running rough intermittently. CHT spiked to 474°F. Re

metadata.pilot_notes: Engine running rough intermittently. CHT spiked to 474°F. Reduced power and enriched mixture. Oil seems to be burning faster than normal.

description: Flight N1156P — KPHX-local — 3.3h — Engine running rough intermittently. CHT spiked to 489°F. Re

description: Flight N1156P — KPHX-KSDL — 1.2h — Rough running throughout flight, couldn't maintain altitude

metadata.pilot_notes: Rough running throughout flight, couldn't maintain altitude at cruise power. CHT very high 483°F, oil pressure low. Declared precautionary and returned to KPHX.

description: Cylinder head temperature on #3 cylinder running 40-60°F above the other cylinders during cruise. First observed three flights ago and trending upward.

metadata.title: Elevated CHT #3
metadata.observation: CHT #3 consistently reaching 430-450°F while CHT #1, #2, #4 remain 360-380°F at same power setting.

description: Engine runs noticeably rough during mag check on left magneto. Right mag check is smooth. RPM drop on left mag is 150 RPM versus 50 RPM on right.

metadata.title: Rough Running on Left Mag
metadata.observation: Mag check at runup: right mag 50 RPM drop (normal), left mag 150 RPM drop with roughness. Cleared after extended runup.

description: CHT readings trending upward over the previous nine flights before failure. Reached 460-480°F on final flights.

metadata.title: Persistently Elevated CHT
metadata.observation: Pilot notes documented persistent high CHT during cruise, particularly on longer flights. Engine leaned aggressively to manage temperatures.

description: Oil consumption increased in the weeks preceding failure. Required adding 1 qt oil every 8-10 flight hours versus normal 15-20 hours.

metadata.title: Increased Oil Consumption
metadata.observation: Pre-flight checks documented oil level dropping faster than historical baseline. No visible external leaks found.

description: Rough engine operation noted intermittently during cruise flight, particularly at lean mixture settings. Cleared when mixture enriched.

metadata.title: Intermittent Rough Running
metadata.observation: Pilot notes on multiple flights mention roughness that cleared with mixture enrichment. Attributed to improper leaning technique at the time.

description: Climb performance noticeably reduced on final flights before failure. Unable to maintain normal cruise RPM at full throttle.

metadata.title: Reduced Power Output
metadata.observation: Pilot reported inability to reach normal cruise RPM. Takeoff roll longer than normal. Climb rate approximately 400 FPM versus normal 770 FPM.

description: Annual inspection completed. All systems checked per FAR 43 Appendix D. Minor discrepancies noted and corrected. Aircraft returned to service.

metadata.mechanic: Cactus Aviation Services — Mike Torres, A&P/IA #3847291
metadata.parts_replaced: Spark plugs rotated and gapped; oil and filter changed

description: Oil and filter change. 50-hour interval. Drained 7 qts 15W-50. Cut filter — trace ferrous particles, within normal limits for this engine variant. Oil analysis sent to Blackstone Labs.

description: 50-hour oil change. OVERDUE — 53.2 hours since last change. Pilot reported oil consumption has increased. Cut filter shows elevated fine metallic particles. RECOMMEND BORESCOPE AND OIL ANALYSIS BEFORE NEXT FLIGHT. Blackstone sample sent.

metadata.mechanic: Desert Aero Maintenance — James Wheeler, A&P/IA #2918473

description: Post-accident teardown inspection following catastrophic engine failure on 2026-03-13. Cylinder #2 connecting rod failed and exited through case. Evidence of chronic lean detonation found: piston crown erosion, cylinder head erosion consistent with detonation, valve face pitting. Engine is NOT REPAIRABLE. Replacement required.

metadata.mechanic: AZ Aviation Overhaul — Linda Chen, A&P/IA #3561028

description: CATASTROPHIC ENGINE FAILURE. Connecting rod failure, rod exited through case. Aircraft made emergency off-airport landing. Aircraft NOT AIRWORTHY. Engine requires replacement.

metadata.mechanic: AZ Aviation Overhaul — Linda Chen, A&P/IA #3561028
